paper_title: Paleoseismic Record Analysis in the Gulf of Corinth

methods.description: This method describes the collection and initial processing of a long piston core from the Gulf of Corinth to study the paleoseismic record.

steps.description: The MD01-2477 long piston core was recovered during a R/V MARION-DUFRESNES cruise as part of the GEOSCIENCES-II program.
steps.searchable_summary: collect piston core from Gulf of Corinth

steps.description: Shipboard processing included GEOTEK core logger profiles and splitting; further laboratory samplings and measurements were done on half cores horizontally stored at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: process core samples with GEOTEK logger and store at 4°C

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of textural parameters and magnetic susceptibility anisotropy to characterize and discriminate homogenites from other fine-grained deposits.

steps.description: To characterize and discriminate homogenites from other fine-grained deposits, analysis of the textural parameters is the most common tool.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze textural parameters to identify homogenites

steps.description: The combination of grain size, undrained shear stress, and magnetic fabric has been used to get a well-constrained characterization of the homogenites and their temporal distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze grain size, shear stress, and magnetic fabric

steps.description: Application of the Magnetic Susceptibility Anisotropy (MSA) in the study and identification of post-depositional disturbances related to earthquake triggering.
steps.searchable_summary: apply MSA to identify post-depositional disturbances

methods.method_name: Age/Depth Curve and Sedimentation Rate Estimation
methods.description: This method involves creating an age/depth curve based on sedimentation rates estimated from hemipelagic thicknesses and applying zero-time to homogenites and turbidites events.

steps.description: The age/depth curve is based on the assumption of sedimentation rates estimated from hemipelagic thicknesses.
steps.searchable_summary: model age/depth curve using hemipelagic thicknesses
